title: cracca secunda
side_effects: Cracca secunda can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and respiratory issues. In severe cases, ingestion of the plant can lead to nausea, vomiting, and stomach cramps, as well as increased heart rate and blood pressure. Prolonged exposure to the plant's sap may cause skin inflammation, redness, and blistering, and in rare instances, anaphylaxis can occur. It is essential to consult a medical professional before using the Cracca secunda plant for any health benefits.
